metadata.name: Expected Manager (you can leave this blank)
Referral Cheat Sheet
metadata.value:
Here is what to consider when thinking about who to refer:
Analytical & Structured Thinking: At JL10,  Candidate can independently scope a problem, identify the critical path, and produce high-quality deliverables with minimal iteration. 
- Look for evidence of work where they structured the problem themselves (not handed a framework), made defensible assumptions under uncertainty, and communicated findings to senior audiences. - Can sufficiently review and provide insight on Cost-benefit models, BVA work, or field-to-insight synthesis""
""Cross-functional Project Management: JL10 expects someone who coordinates independently on behalf of their team and manages deliverables to completion without hand-holding. 
 Look for examples of spanning multiple departments or functions, managing upward to senior leadership, and making judgment calls on when to escalate vs. resolve. The value in this role is the candidates ability to step into a gap and becoming reliable fast""
""Adaptability & Context-Switching:  Should be able to have a largely self-managed work calendar and be comfortable owning their ramp-up in a new context. 
- Required evidence of genuine environment shifts (country, sector, team type) where they became productive quickly and made contributions""
Field & Operations Orientation: Confirm willingness and some prior exposure to operational (non-desk) work and rural / last mile context exposure. At JL10, this person should be comfortable translating observations into recommendations without needing a template to do so.

metadata.value:
Here is what to consider when thinking about who to refer:
Relevant academic background in Agronomy, Soil Science, Plant Nutrition, Microbiology, Agroecology, or a related field.
Demonstrated experience in agricultural research (e.g. experimental design, sampling methodologies, and bias minimisation techniques).
Deep technical knowledge of soil science (biological, chemical and physical), plant nutrition, nutrient management options, soil food web theory, nutrient cycling, and soil health assessment options.
Understanding of soil/plant laboratory operations management and soil chemical, biological and physical analysis techniques (e.g. dry chemistry, microbial enumeration).
Familiarity with various fertiliser types (especially microbial biostimulants and synthetic fertilisers).
Experience with data analysis tools.

metadata.name: Expected Manager (you can leave this blank)
Referral Cheat Sheet
metadata.value:
Here is what to consider when thinking about who to refer:
Commercial orientation and business judgment - Can read margins, costs, pricing, volumes, working capital, and customer / supplier economics. Makes decisions from numbers, not only relationships or narratives. P&L or private-sector exposure is a strong proxy.
Versatility: can strategize, manage, and operate - Can move between strategy, people management, and hands-on execution. Not too senior to operate; not too junior to think structurally. This is probably the most important overall success marker.
Execution ownership in ambiguous, early-stage work - Has built or stabilized something messy without perfect information. Can create structure, make decisions, manage tradeoffs, and push work forward without waiting for perfect clarity.

metadata.name: Expected Manager (you can leave this blank)
Referral Cheat Sheet
metadata.value:
Here is what to consider when thinking about who to refer:
The Technical Assistance Specialist will be joining at an exciting time as we refine our TA strategy and implement our early TA projects. Their support and insights will directly shape this strategy and how we deliver impactful TA, enabling us to transform the lives of millions of farmers and households across our portfolio.

Challenges: The Specialist will have to build rapport with investees to get their buy-in into suggested TA projects/support. This often means physical visits to their premises and understanding their needs before suggesting interventions and working through ambiguous situations and with limited data.

Opportunities: This role will allow you to develop project management, design and stakeholder management skills as you will balance the interests of both the fund and the investees.

location.name: Flexible: Poland, Spain, Portugal, Estonia, Romania, South Africa, and Kenya

metadata.name: Expected Manager (you can leave this blank)
Referral Cheat Sheet
metadata.value:
Here is what to consider when thinking about who to refer:
The AI Engineering Lead is an opportunity to be at the forefront of applying AI and automation to solve real business challenges that directly impact farmers and operations. The successful candidate will spend the majority of their time designing, building, deploying, and scaling AI-powered automations, intelligent workflows, integrations, and decision-support solutions that drive measurable business outcomes. Unlike many AI roles that focus primarily on research or experimentation, this role is focused on practical application and delivery. The candidate will work closely with business leaders to identify opportunities, build solutions that create tangible value, and help grow Tupande's broader AI and automation capability through hands-on coaching and mentorship. Over time, the role is expected to influence how AI is embedded into Tupande's operating model and become a key contributor to Tupande's long-term growth and innovation strategy.

Challenges: This role requires balancing deep technical work with business engagement and capability building. The successful candidate will need to understand complex business processes across multiple functions and translate them into practical AI-powered automations, intelligent workflows, and integrated solutions that deliver measurable value. They will be expected to work across a diverse technology ecosystem, integrating AI/ automation solutions with enterprise systems, operational platforms, and existing business processes while ensuring reliability, scalability, and adoption. As AI and automation continue to evolve rapidly, the role also requires continuous learning and the ability to evaluate which technologies are worth investing in versus those that create unnecessary complexity.

Opportunities: This role provides an opportunity to spend the majority of time building and deploying practical AI and automation solutions that directly impact customers, operations, and business performance. The successful candidate will work on a wide range of challenges, from intelligent workflows and AI-enabled decision support to systems integrations and process automation. They will have the opportunity to influence how AI is adopted across`the organization, help establish best practices for AI and automation engineering, and play a key role in growing Tupande's internal capability. For engineers who enjoy solving real-world problems and seeing their work create tangible business and farmer impact, the role offers a unique combination of technical depth, ownership, and purpose.

metadata.name: Expected Manager (you can leave this blank)
Referral Cheat Sheet
metadata.value:
Here is what to consider when thinking about who to refer:
Seeking an exceptional AI and automation engineering leader to build Tupande's automation capability, lead high-impact solutions, and help scale farmer impact through hands-on engineering, business partnership, and technical leadership.

The Automation Engineering Lead is a rare opportunity to build and lead a new AI and automation engineering capability at scale while remaining deeply involved in the technical work. The successful candidate will help shape how Tupande uses AI, automation solution integration to accelerate its farmer-first agribusiness model and serve millions of farmers more effectively. The candidate will have the opportunity to set technical direction, influence Tupande's strategy, build a high-performing engineering capability, and personally contribute to the design and delivery of high-impact solutions. This role will also shape how automation and AI are leveraged and establish a model that could influence broader advanced technology adoption across the organization.

Challenges: This role is responsible for establishing and scaling a relatively new AI and automation engineering capability within Tupande. The successful candidate will need to balance strategic leadership with hands-on delivery, often making decisions in situations where there is no established blueprint to follow. They will need to quickly develop a deep understanding of Tupande's operating model, customer journeys, and business priorities while identifying where AI, automation, and system integrations can create the greatest value. The role will also require balancing competing priorities, ensuring quality and governance standards are maintained, and building technical capability across the team while simultaneously delivering measurable business outcomes.

Opportunities: This is a unique opportunity to shape how a large agricultural organization uses AI, and automation to accelerate its farmer-first vision. The role offers significant influence over technology strategy, organizational priorities, and future engineering capability. The successful candidate will have the opportunity to build and lead a high-performing engineering function, establish standards and ways of working, and personally contribute to high-impact solutions that improve customer experience, operational efficiency, and farmer impact at scale. Few roles offer the opportunity to combine technical leadership, hands-on engineering, organizational transformation, and direct business influence to this extent.

metadata.value:
Here is what to consider when thinking about who to refer:
"Strong academic or professional experience designing and implementing evaluations  and analysing data  (e.g. with large scale data collection and field team management).
Knowledge of evaluation concepts and mastery of experimental and quasi-experimental evaluation designs.
Strong analytical skills, especially the use of statistical techniques leveraged in impact evaluation measurement strategies (e.g.   propensity score matching, difference in difference, regression discontinuity, etc.).
Knowledge of statistical software, such as  Stata or R).
Strong project management skills with the ability to oversee multiple projects with various levels of complexity at the same time. 
Has a clear and compelling written communication style with an eye for design and visual presentation. 
Ability to engage with multiple partners in a diplomatic manner and have strong relationship building skills. 
Leadership experience, including managing a team. "

metadata.name: Expected Manager (you can leave this blank)
Referral Cheat Sheet
metadata.value:
Here is what to consider when thinking about who to refer:
Commercial & Strategic Acumen: Demonstrated ability to develop commercial strategies and identify significant revenue generation as well as impact opportunities in the agricultural sector for both farmers and the organization

Innovation Pipeline Management: Proven capability in prioritizing, designing, and scaling commercial pilots in a logical and evidence-based sequence.

Financial & Risk Modeling: Strong proficiency in financial modeling and budget management.

Supply Chain & Logistical Execution: Proven capability in overseeing partner negotiations, aggregation logistics, quality control, last mile distribution and ensuring products meet buyer standards or export regulations.

Project & Performance Management: Track record of setting and reviewing KPIs, managing complex multi-workstream programs, and driving accountability for results.

Partnership Management: Demonstrated success in identifying, developing, and managing commercial partnerships with buyers, processors, and distributors.

Stakeholder Influence: Ability to represent the department at senior levels and secure buy-in from cross-functional teams including Field Execution and Government Relations.

People Leadership: Experience coaching senior staff and building a results-oriented, collaborative team culture.
